ground_truth.title: Smartphone Basics for Older Adults: Unlocking the Digital World
ground_truth.author: BCBSOK Connect Team
ground_truth.main_content:
If you're new to smartphones, they can seem scary and confusing. But they don't have to be. Read on for a simple guide to the basics of using a smartphone.

What are smartphone apps? A smartphone is a mobile phone with many of the same functions of a computer, but in the palm of your hand. It uses software applications, or "apps," to do different tasks. You use apps to make phone calls, send text messages, take photos, browse the internet, play games and much more. Some apps are already loaded on your phone when you buy it. You can download other apps to your phone from an online store.

Do you have an Apple iPhone? Only Apple makes iPhones. Apple uses an operating system called iOS. You download apps from the Apple App Store while using your phone.

Do you have an Android smartphone? Lots of companies make smartphones that run on an Android operating system. You can download apps from the Google Play Store.

You interact with your phone by touching or tapping the screen with your fingers or a stylus. Camera - Most smartphones have a built-in camera app to take photos, selfies and videos.

Each icon stands for a different app. Tapping an icon will open the app.
